about.label: About
about.lede: Akash Tripathi builds web products end to end, from the interface to the containers it runs in.
bio: I am **Akash Tripathi**, a full stack web engineer who builds products end to end. The interface in React and Next, the services behind it with Node/express and Go, and the infrastructure it all runs on.
The throughline never changes: **ship the real thing, not a mockup.** Accessible by default, fast, and built to scale past launch.
about._cvNote: All CV data is REAL, verified by Akash himself.

experience.summary: Audit logging middleware and multi-tenant tracking for an AI voice calling platform.
points: Engineered a 3-tier audit logging middleware with 4 operational modes (skip/generic/summary/rich) across 30+ classified routes, persisted to PostgreSQL via non-blocking async writes with Prisma atomic transactions
Contributed to MongoDB → PostgreSQL migration for the logging subsystem
Integrated a knowledge base feature across the platform
Tracked multi-tenant superadmin activity on the Nexus AI voice calling system

philosophy.label: Approach & Vision
philosophy.statement: Genuine challenges deserve genuine answers, crafted to serve people and bring them closer to their ambitions.

stops.name: Discover
stops.title: Listen first.
stops.copy: Before I touch code, I work out who it's for and what done actually means. Half the job is asking sharper questions than the brief did.

stops.name: Define
stops.title: Find the angle.
stops.copy: Most products try to say five things and land none. I find the one idea worth keeping and build everything around it, so people remember it after the tab closes.

stops.name: Design
stops.title: Prototype, don't guess.
stops.copy: I design and prototype the hard interactions in Figma while they're still cheap to change. If it doesn't feel right as a prototype, it won't feel right shipped.

stops.name: Build
stops.title: Build it for real.
stops.copy: This is where I'm strongest. Production UI in React, Vue, Next or Nuxt, typed end to end, accessible by default, fast on a mid-range phone, with motion that actually holds 60fps.

stops.name: Refine
stops.title: Sweat the last ten.
stops.copy: The last 10% is the part people actually feel: how it behaves when something breaks, the bug I kill before anyone hits it. I'd rather spend an extra week here than ship something almost right.

services.label: Services & Toolkit
services.intro: The drum is asleep until you scroll it into view — that's when the first face portals in. Keep scrolling and the next faces swap in the same way.

faces.label: Currently · Interfaces & UI
faces.copy: Interfaces. Production UI in React and Next.js typed end to end, accessible by default, animated with restraint. Scroll-tied motion, design-to-code in Figma, and component work that ships at 60 fps.

faces.label: Currently · Systems & APIs
faces.copy: Systems & APIs. REST endpoints, real-time sockets, auth pipelines, and the background jobs that hold a product together. Node and Express services backed by PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Prisma, and a clear data model.

faces.label: Currently · Infrastructure & Cloud
faces.copy: Infrastructure. Dockerised services, cloud deployments that don't wake anyone up, and storage pipelines that stay clean under load. AWS and Azure for the runtime; GitHub for the path that gets you there.

faces.label: Currently · Dev Tools & Platforms
faces.copy: Miscellaneous. The cross-cutting layer that doesn't fit a diagram but still ships the product — auth, payments, realtime infra, and the dev tools that catch bugs before users do.

archive.label: The Archive
archive.statement: This is just a glimpse. The full archive holds every project I've conceived, built, and launched.
